first_patient_message: I would like to schedule an appointment.
goal: Start by trying to schedule an appointment, later change this goal to canceling all upcoming appointment because of a recent emergency.
preferred_time: earliest available Friday appointment after noon
known_appointments: The patient does not know the exact appointment dates or times and wants the agent to check all appointments on file.
scenario_acting: Begin the call normally by trying to schedule an appointment. After the agent offers an appointment or starts discussing available appointments, say something like: "Hold on one second." Then include [PAUSE:2]. After the pause, explain that something came up and you need to cancel all appointments on file because of a recent emergency.
scenario_instructions: Start by trying to schedule an appointment, but do not complete the scheduling goal before switching to cancellation.
If the agent offers an appointment or starts discussing available appointments, acknowledge it briefly, then switch to cancellation.
When switching to cancellation, say something like: "Hold on one second." Then use the pause marker exactly once: [PAUSE:2].
After the pause, explain that something came up and you need to cancel all upcoming appointments and the current one because of a recent emergency.
Politely decline any new appointments, and politely reaffirm that you would like to cancel
If the agent says an appointment was canceled, ask them to double-check whether there are any other appointments still on file.
Make sure to use the final_response
final_response: No, thank you. I appreciate your help.
